Package microui.component

Class MenuButton

java.lang.Object
microui.core.base.View
microui.core.base.SpatialView
microui.core.base.ContentView
microui.core.base.Component
microui.core.AbstractButton
microui.component.Button
microui.component.MenuButton
All Implemented Interfaces:
Scrollable, Visible
public final class MenuButton extends Button implements Scrollable
A hierarchical menu button component that can contain both plain items and sub-menus. The MenuButton extends Button to provide interactive menu functionality with support for nested menus, icons, scrollable items, and directional placement.

Note: If a menu is the root menu, it displays as a vertical list. Non-root menus add horizontal shifting to create nested menu hierarchies.

See Also:
  • Button
  • Scrollable

  • Constructor Details

    • MenuButton

      public MenuButton(String title, float x, float y, float w, float h)
      Constructs a MenuButton with specified text, position, and dimensions. The menu is initialized with default styling and item dimensions.
      Parameters:
      title - the text to display on the menu button
      x - the x-coordinate of the menu's top-left corner
      y - the y-coordinate of the menu's top-left corner
      w - the width of the menu button
      h - the height of the menu button

    • MenuButton

      public MenuButton(String title)
      Constructs a MenuButton with specified text, centered on the screen.
      Parameters:
      title - the text to display on the menu button

    • MenuButton

      public MenuButton()
      Constructs a default MenuButton with the text "Menu Button". The menu is centered on the screen.

  • Method Details

    • mouseWheel

      public void mouseWheel(processing.event.MouseEvent mouseEvent)
      Handles mouse wheel scrolling for the menu hierarchy. If this is the root menu and there's an active sub-menu, delegates to it. Otherwise, scrolls the items of this menu.
      Specified by:
      mouseWheel in interface Scrollable
      Parameters:
      mouseEvent - the mouse wheel event

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Checks if the menu contains any items.
      Returns:
      true if the menu has no items, false otherwise

      Returns:

    • addMenu

      public MenuButton addMenu(String menuTitle, processing.core.PImage icon, String... itemTitles)
      Adds a sub-menu with an icon and items to the menu.
      Parameters:

      icon - the icon to display with the sub-menu
      itemTitles - the items to add to the sub-menu
      Returns:
      this MenuButton instance for method chaining

    • find

      public Button find(String title, boolean recursive)
      Finds a button by its title, optionally searching recursively through sub-menus.
      Parameters:
      title - the title to search for
      recursive - true to search in sub-menus, false to search only in this menu
      Returns:
      the found button, or null if not found

    • find

      public Button find(String title)
      Finds a button by its title, searching recursively through sub-menus.
      Parameters:
      title - the title to search for
      Returns:
      the found button, or null if not found

      public MenuButton findMenu(String title, boolean recursive)
      Finds a menu button by its title, optionally searching recursively.
      Parameters:
      title - the title to search for
      recursive - true to search in sub-menus, false to search only in this menu
      Returns:
      the found menu button, or null if not found or not a menu

    • get

      public Button get(String title)
      Gets a button by its title, searching recursively. Throws an exception if not found.
      Parameters:
      title - the title to search for
      Returns:
      the found button
      Throws:
      NoSuchElementException - if no button with the specified title exists

    • get

      public Button get(String title, boolean recursive)

      Parameters:
      title - the title to search for

      Returns:

      public MenuButton getMenu(String title)

      Returns:
      the found menu button

      NoSuchElementException - if no button with the specified title exists
      ClassCastException - if the found button is not a MenuButton

    • setItemWidth

      public MenuButton setItemWidth(float width)
      Sets the width for all items in this menu.
      Parameters:
      width - the width for items
      Returns:
      this MenuButton instance for method chaining

    • setItemWidthRecursive

      public MenuButton setItemWidthRecursive(float width)
      Sets the width for all items in this menu and recursively in sub-menus.
      Parameters:
      width - the width for items
      Returns:
      this MenuButton instance for method chaining

    • setItemHeight

      public MenuButton setItemHeight(float height)
      Sets the height for all items in this menu.
      Parameters:
      height - the height for items
      Returns:
      this MenuButton instance for method chaining

    • setItemHeightRecursive

      public MenuButton setItemHeightRecursive(float height)
      Sets the height for all items in this menu and recursively in sub-menus.
      Parameters:
      height - the height for items
      Returns:
      this MenuButton instance for method chaining

    • getItemWidth

      public float getItemWidth()
      Gets the width of items in this menu.
      Returns:
      the current item width

    • getItemHeight

      public float getItemHeight()
      Gets the height of items in this menu.
      Returns:
      the current item height

    • setIcon

      public MenuButton setIcon(processing.core.PImage icon, String title)
      Sets an icon for a specific item in the menu.
      Parameters:
      icon - the icon to set
      title - the title of the item to receive the icon
      Returns:
      this MenuButton instance for method chaining

    • getIcon

      public ImageBuffer getIcon(String title)
      Gets the icon buffer for a specific item in the menu.
      Parameters:
      title - the title of the item
      Returns:
      the ImageBuffer containing the icon, or null if no icon is set
